segments.text: We have heard with our ears, O God, Our fathers have told us, The deeds You did in their days, In days of old:
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: You drove out the nations with Your hand, But them You planted; You afflicted the peoples, and cast them out.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For they did not gain possession of the land by their own sword, Nor did their own arm save them;
But it was Your right hand, Your arm, and the light of Your countenance, Because You favored them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You are my King, O God; Command victories for Jacob.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Through You we will push down our enemies; Through Your name we will trample those who rise up against us.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: For I will not trust in my bow, Nor shall my sword save me.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: But You have saved us from our enemies, And have put to shame those who hated us.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: In God we boast all day long, And praise Your name forever. Selah
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: But You have cast us off and put us to shame, And You do not go out with our armies.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You make us turn back from the enemy, And those who hate us have taken spoil for themselves.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You have given us up like sheep intended for food, And have scattered us among the nations.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You sell Your people for next to nothing, And are not enriched by selling them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You make us a reproach to our neighbors, A scorn and a derision to those all around us.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You make us a byword among the nations, A shaking of the head among the peoples.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: My dishonor is continually before me, And the shame of my face has covered me,
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Because of the voice of him who reproaches and reviles, Because of the enemy and the avenger.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: All this has come upon us; But we have not forgotten You, Nor have we dealt falsely with Your covenant.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Our heart has not turned back, Nor have our steps departed from Your way;
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: But You have severely broken us in the place of jackals, And covered us with the shadow of death.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: If we had forgotten the name of our God, Or stretched out our hands to a foreign god,
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Would not God search this out? For He knows the secrets of the heart.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Yet for Your sake we are killed all day long; We are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Awake! Why do You sleep, O Lord? Arise! Do not cast us off forever.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Why do You hide Your face, And forget our affliction and our oppression?
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: For our soul is bowed down to the dust; Our body clings to the ground.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Arise for our help, And redeem us for Your mercies' sake.
segments.speaker: Psalmist
